reasoning_model_1: The model's content accuracy is perfect; it reads the requested sentence 'You're back? Why didn't you tell me!' verbatim without any additions or omissions. In terms of the 'Emotion' dimension, the audio exhibits a high level of naturalness and expressiveness. At 00:00-00:01, the pitch rises sharply on 'You're back?', effectively conveying genuine surprise. The following phrase 'Why didn't you tell me!' (00:01-00:02) maintains the high energy and fast pace typical of a startled reaction. The tone is spot-on, sounding like a human spontaneously reacting to an unexpected arrival.
reasoning_model_2: The model fails the Content Accuracy criterion significantly. While it does read the target sentence between 00:00 and 00:02, it hallucinates and adds two complete sentences that were not in the prompt ('I can't believe it. I was so sure you'd stayed away forever.') from 00:02 to 00:06. Regarding the 'Emotion' dimension, the target segment (00:00-00:02) does sound surprised, but the vocal quality is somewhat breathy and exaggerated, akin to a stylized anime character rather than a natural human interaction. The addition of unrequested text violates the user's specific command to 'read this sentence', constituting a major issue.
model_comparison: Model 1 is the clear winner. It followed the instructions perfectly, delivering the exact text with a highly natural and convincing surprised emotion. Model 2 failed the content constraints by adding significant unrequested text (hallucination). Furthermore, Model 1's vocal delivery felt more grounded and realistic compared to the slightly stylized/cartoony delivery of Model 2.
